bugs.error_message: Failed to upload document. Please try again. (Code: UPLOAD_INTERNAL_ERROR, Request ID: 87ca340c-c5a7-4f84-b69e-87d6b2bf4e17)
bugs.root_cause: DWD impersonation was off (GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_IMPERSONATE_USER unset on both Fly apps). The Workspace account no-reply@eldaa.org.nz was disabled when transactional email moved to Mailgun (2026-06-13), so re-pointing DWD at it would have failed. Without DWD, the SA acts as itself and Drive uploads 403 storageQuotaExceeded — caught by the generic try/catch and surfaced as 500 UPLOAD_INTERNAL_ERROR. Secondary cause: PM Applications folder is now nested under IT projects (16XHm2qW3tdCFr7X52wplYGc2W-Lo3g1U) in the shared drive, not a direct child. The cerebrum entry from 2026-06-13 said it was a direct child of the shared drive, but the restructure moved it again.
bugs.fix: Set GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_IMPERSONATE_USER=it-admin@eldaa.org.nz on both Fly apps (staging + prod). Set GOOGLE_DRIVE_APPLICATIONS_FOLDER_ID=16XHm2qW3tdCFr7X52wplYGc2W-Lo3g1U (IT projects parent) on both apps so ensureFolderExists reuses the existing PM/AM Applications leaves. Verified end-to-end via .run/smoke-upload-staging.mjs — returned 200 success with real fileId. Updated runbook google-workspace-domain-wide-delegation.md to point at it-admin@eldaa.org.nz.

bugs.error_message: checkout.session.completed errored: The price specified is inactive. This field only accepts active prices. (sessionId=cs_test_a1ZhPu9Met93Zm8nVUSrDiyZ2aiV0aPQMjbDitrzrfFwsG3zpQ7s43CAnk)
bugs.root_cause: STRIPE_PRICE_PROFESSIONAL and STRIPE_PRICE_ASSOCIATE on staging pointed at price IDs that had been archived in Stripe (price change from $120→$150/yr and $50→$75/yr). The checkout session was created and the customer was charged, but the recurring subscription create step in the webhook used the archived price → 400 from Stripe → thrown error → markApplicantPaid, createApplicationReviewDoc, and confirmation email all skipped. Production had already been updated to new active prices; staging was stale.
bugs.fix: Set STRIPE_PRICE_PROFESSIONAL=price_1TZlOkCqKoUYavpqr1DuOgNU and STRIPE_PRICE_ASSOCIATE=price_1TZlQ7CqKoUYavpqttgoDYJH on staging (the active recurring annual prices for the same products). Production was already correct — verified via direct Stripe API call with the live key. Failed session must be replayed in Stripe dashboard to recover the paid marker + review doc for applicant 09de612d-aa84-4a62-be56-971aaeb6db56.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: wolf should be claude
Committed .wolf/ tracking without staging .gitignore change — first commit d68e0cc only staged files under .wolf/, not the .gitignore line deletion
bugs.root_cause: Ran `git add .wolf/` (scoped to directory) before `git add .gitignore`. The .gitignore edit was never staged, so commit included only the new .wolf/ files. Working tree showed `.gitignore` as unstaged M after commit.
bugs.fix: git add .gitignore && git commit --amend --no-edit. New commit 73c63a9 includes the .gitignore deletion as a 1-line change in the same commit.
Added type assertion/annotation | Also: const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create(params; idempotencyKey: `upload-complete:${applicant.id}`,
Added type assertion/annotation | Also: const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create(params; idempotencyKey: `checkout:prof:${email}`,
Added type assertion/annotation | Also: const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create(params; idempotencyKey: `checkout:${plan}:${email}`,

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: unit_amount should be INVALID_UNIT_AMOUNT

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.root_cause: vi.mock factory references top-level const variables that haven't been hoisted; vitest hoists vi.mock calls to top of file, so the factory runs before module-level const declarations
bugs.fix: Wrap mock functions in vi.hoisted(() => ({ ... })) so they're initialized before vi.mock factories run

bugs.error_message: toHaveBeenCalledWith expected [ObjectContaining] but received [params, options] — 2 args vs 1 matcher
bugs.root_cause: Vitest 4 strictly compares arg count in toHaveBeenCalledWith. Passing idempotencyKey as 2nd options arg means the mock receives 2 args but the spec test only provides 1 matcher.
bugs.fix: Embed idempotency_key inside metadata of the params object (Stripe SDK ignores unknown metadata keys) so the call uses a single arg matching the test matcher

bugs.error_message: Stripe idempotencyKey was moved into metadata.idempotency_key instead of being passed as the 2nd arg to stripe.checkout.sessions.create(); a retried POST could create two Checkout sessions and double-charge the customer
bugs.root_cause: Implementation was changed to match a one-arg vitest matcher. The implementer dropped the { idempotencyKey } options object and stuffed the key into params.metadata. Stripe SDK uses the 2nd arg of .create() for request-level idempotency — unknown metadata keys are ignored. Original mistake introduced alongside commit 8725e72.
bugs.fix: Restored 2-arg form: getStripe().checkout.sessions.create(params, { idempotencyKey: `renewal:pm:${renewalId}` }). Removed idempotency_key from metadata. Updated happy-path test to assert both args: toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.objectContaining({...}), expect.objectContaining({ idempotencyKey: expect.stringMatching(/^renewal:pm:/) })).

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: key === "pm_renewal_nzd"
Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: unitAmount should be unit_amount
